agent_output: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Sprint 1: Implement basic webhook delivery. A WebhookDeliveryService that sends an event payload via HTTP POST to a subscriber's registered URL and reports whether delivery succeeded or failed. No retry logic, no backoff, no subscription suspension — those are Sprint 2 concerns.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"A webhook is delivered via HTTP POST to the subscriber's registered URL with the event payload as the JSON body","testable_assertion":"Every delivery attempt issues an HTTP POST (not GET, PUT, etc.) to the exact subscriber URL, with Content-Type application/json and the event payload serialized as the JSON body"},{"rule":"Delivery is successful if and only if the subscriber responds with an HTTP 2xx status within 5 seconds","testable_assertion":"Any 2xx response (200, 201, 202, 204, etc.) received within 5 seconds results in status 'delivered'; any non-2xx response OR a timeout beyond 5 seconds results in status 'failed'"},{"rule":"Non-2xx responses are delivery failures","testable_assertion":"A 503, 400, 500, or any other non-2xx status code results in status 'failed'"}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"Single service: WebhookDeliveryService with method deliver(event, subscriberUrl) returning DeliveryResult","implementation_note":"DeliveryResult is a simple value object with two fields: status ('delivered' | 'failed') and http_status_code (int or null if timeout/network error). No enum beyond these two status values for Sprint 1."},{"decision":"HTTP client timeout set to 5 seconds","implementation_note":"The 5-second timeout is a domain rule, not an infrastructure default. Make it a named constant (e.g., DELIVERY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 5) so it reads as an intentional domain decision."},{"decision":"Leave extension points for retry but do NOT implement retry logic","implementation_note":"The deliver() method should return the DeliveryResult to its caller without any retry. Sprint 2 will wrap this service with a RetryScheduler (BullMQ). Do not add retry parameters, retry counts, or queue dependencies. A clean separation where deliver() is a single-attempt operation is the extension point."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"Successful delivery","given":"A webhook event and a valid subscriber URL","when":"The subscriber responds with HTTP 200 OK within 5 seconds","then":"deliver() returns DeliveryResult with status 'delivered' and http_status_code 200"},{"criterion":"Failed delivery on non-2xx","given":"A webhook event and a subscriber URL","when":"The subscriber responds with HTTP 503","then":"deliver() returns DeliveryResult with status 'failed' and http_status_code 503"},{"criterion":"Payload sent as JSON POST","given":"A webhook event","when":"deliver() is called","then":"An HTTP POST is made to the subscriber URL with the event payload as the JSON body and Content-Type application/json"},{"criterion":"Timeout treated as failure","given":"A webhook event and a subscriber URL","when":"The subscriber does not respond within 5 seconds","then":"deliver() returns DeliveryResult with status 'failed' and http_status_code null (or equivalent sentinel indicating no response)"}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"delivered","technical_term":"DeliveryResult.status = 'delivered'","definition":"The subscriber returned an HTTP 2xx response within the 5-second window"},{"domain_term":"failed","technical_term":"DeliveryResult.status = 'failed'","definition":"The subscriber returned a non-2xx response, or did not respond within 5 seconds, or a network error occurred"},{"domain_term":"subscriber URL","technical_term":"subscriberUrl: string parameter","definition":"The HTTP endpoint registered by the subscriber to receive webhook POSTs"}],"contradictions_resolved":[],"open_questions":["Should http_status_code be null or a distinct sentinel (e.g., -1) when a timeout or network error occurs? Bird's ACs only cover explicit HTTP responses. Shaq should pick null and document it; Bird can refine in review.","Bird's ACs don't specify required HTTP headers beyond the implicit JSON content type. Shaq should set Content-Type: application/json at minimum and flag if other headers (e.g., signature, idempotency key) are expected in Sprint 2."]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":92,"high_confidence_areas":["Domain rules are clear and directly testable","Bird and MJ are fully aligned on Sprint 1 scope","DeliveryResult shape is agreed upon","Sprint 2 boundary is explicit — no retry, no backoff, no suspension"],"low_confidence_areas":["Timeout/network-error representation in DeliveryResult (not specified by either agent)","HTTP headers beyond Content-Type (not discussed)"],"assumptions":["Sprint 1 is greenfield — no existing webhook infrastructure to integrate with","The event payload is already a serializable object; serialization format details are Shaq's discretion","No authentication or signature verification on the outbound POST is required for Sprint 1"]}}

No enum beyond these two status values for Sprint 1."},{"decision":"HTTP client timeout set to 5 seconds","implementation_note":"The 5-second timeout is a domain rule, not an infrastructure default. Make it a named constant (e.g., DELIVERY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 5) so it reads as an intentional domain decision."},{"decision":"Leave extension points for retry but do NOT implement retry logic","implementation_note":"The deliver() method should return the DeliveryResult to its caller without any retry. A clean separation where deliver() is a single-attempt operation is the extension point."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"Successful delivery","given":"A webhook event and a valid subscriber URL","when":"The subscriber responds with HTTP 200 OK within 5 seconds","then":"deliver() returns DeliveryResult with status 'delivered' and http_status_code 200"},{"criterion":"Failed delivery on non-2xx","given":"A webhook event and a subscriber URL","when":"The subscriber responds with HTTP 503","then":"deliver() returns DeliveryResult with status 'failed' and http_status_code 503"},{"criterion":"Payload sent as JSON POST","given":"A webhook event","when":"deliver() is called","then":"An HTTP POST is made to the subscriber URL with the event payload as the JSON body and Content-Type application/json"},{"criterion":"Timeout treated as failure","given":"A webhook event and a subscriber URL","when":"The subscriber does not respond within 5 seconds","then":"deliver() returns DeliveryResult with status 'failed' and http_status_code null (or equivalent sentinel indicating no response)"}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"delivered","technical_term":"DeliveryResult.status = 'delivered'","definition":"The subscriber returned an HTTP 2xx response within the 5-second window"},{"domain_term":"failed","technical_term":"DeliveryResult.status = 'failed'","definition":"The subscriber returned a non-2xx response, or did not respond within 5 seconds, or a network error occurred"},{"domain_term":"subscriber URL","technical_term":"subscriberUrl: string parameter","definition":"The HTTP endpoint registered by the subscriber to receive webhook POSTs"}],"contradictions_resolved":[],"open_questions":["Should http_status_code be null or a distinct sentinel (e.g., -1) when a timeout or network error occurs? Bird's ACs only cover explicit HTTP responses. Shaq should pick null and document it; Bird can refine in review.","Bird's ACs don't specify required HTTP headers beyond the implicit JSON content type.
